transcript.text: What's new in your world?
Not much.
It's like winter has just descended on us full force.
Oh, yeah.
It's like a blizzard outside right now.
I was thinking about wading home from my co-working space through like six inches of snow.
Oh, geez.
That's wild.
That is wild.
We're skiers, though, so it's good news.
Yeah, maybe just trying to ski in home instead of walking.
Yeah, exactly.
Honestly, people do.
They cross-country ski.
bring their skis to work.
That's so crazy.
We're right on a bike path, so you can like ski along Lake Champlain, kind of the whole length of the city.
Wow.
You can ski to work.
That sounds like a place I hope I never have to see you with my own two eyes.
I'm going to take your word for it.
That's what my mother said.
All right.
And is it just the two of us today?
Should I jump in?
It is, yeah.
Steve wanted people to join.
I told them all to film in.
You guys have a lot going on.
Yeah, there's definitely some new stuff.
Okay.
So I'll just jump in.
feel like a lot of it's just kind of updates and thinking about timelines and kind of next steps.
For ops, so I saw that Steve fixed the logic flows, or he's in the process of fixing of them, which is awesome.
So one of the things I wanted to do with this strategy refresh is they're going to build a new pipeline to produce content.
And I was hoping we could use this opportunity, since we have the engineering support, to build those logic workflows in in a more automated way.
But I didn't want to do that until you all felt confident that it was sort of like working the way you wanted it to, obviously.
Because then going back and having to manually tweak it's annoying, or we'll have to put in a ticket for engineering support.
It's like not the biggest.

But it feels like if you're going to have to change anything, it would make more sense, especially if you feel like you're going to have an answer soon, then it might make sense for me to wait and put it in the So this has been fixed and it should be working fine now.

I don't expect we're going to see any change in behavior or how we want to approach this, unless for some reason it breaks again, which if it does, we probably want to restore it back to how it works currently.

So all that is to say that you should be fine, we should be good to like bake this into some automated structure that the engineering team provides, and it should continue to work just sort of normally.
Okay, awesome.

And then my other question was, we'd sort of been doing it in a manual way based on this documentation, and you had sort of highlighted which ones were priorities to include, and then, you know, you had the logic doc that used the article text to decide what workflow made most sense.
We've

I'm wondering, given your shift in positioning, if some of that shift in priority might have changed, too, like, if you wanted to revisit either of those docs, because that's basically what I'll hand off to engineering and say this is the logic we're to determine this.
But if there's updates there, then if we update those, then they can kind of use that.
That's a good question.
Let me look into this and get back to you.
I hadn't, like, considered how this might impact which templates we might want to, like, recommend to folks, which workflows.
I would expect that they're going to be very minimal, if any change whatsoever, but I'll look into this and get back to you this week for sure.
Okay, perfect.

And then, yeah, as soon as we sort of have that figured out, we can sort of start, we can build and test the new pipeline that would include the FAQ and the logic workflow and potentially auto-publishing as sort of new steps to make the whole process a lot more streamlined.
Okay, sweet.
Okay.
So thank you for taking a look at the artifact docs we created.
think all of the feedback was really straightforward.
My only question was sort of I saw you had removed where we talked about it as a document to production platform.
And I thought this is the sort of the text that you had added at one point, maybe to the writing guidelines.
And I wanted to just, because it feels like so critical to make sure you're happy with this, just because it'll appear everywhere in all the articles, right?
Like when we're like, logic is this, here's how we define ourselves.
So I just wanted to call this out and make sure that you were kind of comfortable and on board with this language as sort of how we define logic.
Okay.
Yeah, it's a really good question.
My brain wants it to be like AI.
AI spec to production API platform, but that's a little bit long.

And so I think maybe description to production API platform, I had played around with like maybe the AI infrastructure platform that feels like not inaccurate, but not like the best concise summation of like what we do that's different and unique.
Like maybe it is like a description or spec to production API platform.
Let me toy with this as well and get back to you within the next day or two.
I want to just kind of spend some time thinking about this and bounce it off the folks internally as well.
That makes sense.
And I think that was really the only open question.
But yeah, because it will appear everywhere.
I just want to make sure that, you know, you're all sort of on board with how you define yourself.
Yeah, yeah, I know.
And like there's like unrelated to this.
I don't think we'll do this anytime soon, but there's like this movement in the product marketing world, which is like rather than.
Talk about the outcome of what you help books accomplish, like talk about what you are first and foremost on your homepage.
So then there's folks that are doing like, you know, the one size fits all platform for, you know, B2B or B2C consumers.

It's like sales self-labeling, like what they are and who they're for, as opposed to, you know, like our website right now says like, be AI native, like what we're going to help you accomplish as like a, you know, second order benefit.
But, and so a lot is to say, I think like us being really crisp on this will be really helpful.
I'm super for the idea of inserting this at a high level in articles.
I just don't know what exactly you should say just yet.
Yeah, no, that makes sense.
Yeah, and it's interesting from a marketing perspective.
It feels like there are these swings.
Yeah.
Like all the time about being like more outcome focused between, I don't know, like you swing back and forth in marketing.
A hundred percent, a hundred percent.
And I think even within a company within like from quarter to quarter folks will shift.
Back and forth on this.
like, for instance, like our tagline was very briefly like ship AI products faster or ship AI products in minutes and not like quarters or months or weeks.
And I was talking to a CTO who I've shown this to earlier this week, or I guess over the weekend.
And then he was like, oh, so are you guys kind of like cursor?
Will you help me ship really fast?
I'm like, no, no, not quite.
It's a little bit different.
So it's like when you talk only about benefits, there's like 25 different ways you might accomplish that benefit.
And so it doesn't actually help folks understand, like, what are they getting when they buy you?
So all that is to say that there's work to be done there.
Yeah, 100%.
Okay, so that makes sense.
I'll follow up with you on that.
And then for the product and features, the only thing I had questions about, like, there are some places where, like, it talks, like, about a Slack integration coming soon.
And I was wondering if you wanted us to remove any mention of that.
That, or if there were, or if you wanted to go the other way, if there were upcoming product features on your roadmap that you did want to mention, you know, we could include those as well.
I think maybe it's best to remove the Slack integration mentions coming soon.
I think that's a good call.
We are very clear that we want to build this.
We just don't know when exactly it's going to be.
It's kind of just based on how many customers are interested in this.
And so what I don't want to have happen is an article that's three months old that says this, and someone's reading it, and it's still not there.
It's still just coming soon.
And so maybe it's best that it's not put public roadmap in a blog post in this way where it's not where it's go.
I think that makes sense.
And then the question I had around personas was thinking about prioritization of the personas.
You know, my assumption is the sort of CTO persona is primary, and in general, like...
A lot of the focus of the content is going to be on them.
And then secondary would be sort of the more engineering persona, the person who's using Logic.
And then the sort of third lowest priority is the sort of CEO enablement lane of content, where we're speaking to CEOs looking to like shop for their tech teams, essentially.
Yeah, I think that sounds exactly right.
Okay.
So in terms of documents to review, which we don't have to do live right now, but there were a couple other things that I wanted to get your eyes on as well.

One was just to validate our list of competitors to make sure that we're not missing anybody who's either competing, I think, directly or even like in a more adjacent space or even sort of just for like organic traffic.
Because this is who, when we.
We do keyword research.
A lot of what we do is identify keyword gaps between you and competitors and see if there are opportunities to kind of claim specific keywords or a specific space.
Yeah.
And it's also going be the link to this.
Yeah, please do.
Yeah.
There are going to be some changes we'll want to make here, though, yeah.
Okay.

And then the other one is something that we haven't had for all client accounts, but I think given at the beginning, like it was really helpful to focus on how we positioned Logic and especially in these comparison articles, right?

Like if we do another round of comparison articles, understanding how we are now positioning Logic against the different competitor, like competitor tools and these sort of like more adjacent tools and just categories in general.
So I will share this with you as well, just to get your gut check to make sure.
When we do that comparison content, that we're approaching it in the right way.
And my plan would be to add this and create a separate artifact.
So it will do a better job with that kind of...
that's a really good call because there's definitely some nuance here that I think it's really critical that we get right.
Sort of like the TLDR, I think the first part is right.
With Category 1, we see ourselves as complementary, not direct competitors.
And it's often a helpful way for like, you know, more technical ops people who aren't quite developers to use something like Logic is through a tool like Zapier and then.
Linkchain, Llama Index, like Haystack, these are the folks that are what we call AI slash LLM frameworks.
I think the framing we want to probably go with here is what we have here.
I would put it maybe slightly differently as like, you don't actually need, you know, 80% of what Llama Index offers.
And so...
Maybe someday you'll have more advanced use cases, in which case, feel free to build custom using them.
But what you actually need right now, much vast majority of it, you can get with just one minute of work.
You don't need to go through the hassle of building all this stuff.
So I wouldn't say that we deliver a LinkedIn level of capability.
It's just like we deliver 80% of the capability with 1% of the effort is how I think about it.
Yeah, that's really helpful because that would kind of change the...
Yeah, because what I don't want to do is like trigger like some CTOs, like BS sensor, which would like I think reasonably go off in this case, where it's like actually you don't do the same stuff.
I can't like customize which model I send like a specific type of request to or build my own like vector databases.
Like, yeah, we can't do all that stuff, but you don't need to is kind of our point.
I wonder if we're sort of talking about that, you know, Logic does 80%.
Are there specific...
Like, how would you want us to focus when we talk about that 80%?
Is there sort of a general way to talk about it versus the 20% you don't do?
I want to, like, toy with that in writing and find a framing that, again, doesn't feel, like, hand-wavy or BSE.
Yeah.
That makes sense.
Yeah.
then, yeah, I think the last category, think there's only three, were these sort of cloud AI services.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I think it's sort of the same thing.
It's, like, you still have to build everything.
I think I would put, like, the competitive positioning for category two and category three as quite similar.
Yeah.
Okay.
So, I will share this with you to take, like, a more specific look at it.
And then we can update that and turn that into a new artifact as well.
Yeah.
Please.
That sounds good.
Okay.
So, in terms of, like, your new edit...
Editorial strategy, that I have pretty much ready to go.
I wanted to, I feel confident that it's the right direction, but I would like our directors to review it since we're moving into a more technical space.
I wanted another set of eyes on it just to make sure when it comes to things like keyword research, we're not missing any opportunities there.
So they are reviewing and we'll have that to you, I'm hoping tomorrow, but definitely this week to kind of review.
And when you say editorial strategy, like what specifically do you mean here?
So that'll basically be like our approach, like the new topic clusters, our approach to keyword research, sort of how all of this is playing out when it comes to actual article creation.
And updating things like KPIs, what kind of movement we're expecting to see against your goals in terms of both search and AI visibility.
So updating, it'll follow the same format as your current editorial strategy, which is in Notion now.
It'll just be.
Kind of updated, which I can pull up if you want to see it, too.
Okay.
And then updated logic documents.
talked about this.

And then just to give you sort of a sense of what the next steps are and when to expect them, I think if we can get the logic workflows, if you can get those updates to me this week, we can kind of create and start testing that new pipeline.
I think because all we need is the artifacts to start testing those.
And I think those are close enough to get rolling.
We, I think also once you think about how you want to be defined, that sort of like logic is X, I think we can go ahead and start updating existing articles.
I don't think we need to wait for the new strategy for that either.
And so the goal of that would be to, I think we would take a look at existing articles and it feels like if, if there are.
Keywords that would feel really useful to capture, we might recommend rewriting some articles against the new strategy.

And if there are ones that seem like they don't really fit with the current strategy, but we don't want to sunset them, then we would just go through and, like, update those conclusions with the CTA to make it clearer based on the new positioning and kind of what logic is and does.
Sounds great.
And then, yeah, the strategy and topic clusters will be confirmed this week.
And then in terms of what would come next week, once that's all done, that's when we can audit existing keywords to figure out if we should be rewriting or reframing content.
We'll do new keyword research and then present new topics.
And then we'll also update your Scrunch dashboard because we'll want to obviously update all those prompts that we're tracking.
And then also update your Looker.

And then either hopefully next week or maybe the following, depending on when all the sets get done, then we'll sort of run, I think, a couple calibration articles to kind of just recalibrate on this.
But we were getting such good results from the last pipeline that I'm hoping there won't be too much calibration to do.
Sounds good.
Thank you.
Yeah, I'm looking forward to all this.
I appreciate y'all accompanying us on this engine direction.
Well, you know, it's exciting.
I feel like when you're a startup, like you can, you're just so nimble, like you can pivot so quickly compared to some, you know, other companies.
kind of the competitive advantage, yeah.
Yeah, exactly.
So it's exciting.
It's cool to be able to like see an opportunity and be able to just pivot to capture it.
So we're happy to be here on this journey with you.
I wanted to, I think I might have mentioned this last week that Cortex will be closed for that Christmas to New Year's week.
Yeah, I expect we will be as well.
I figured you would be too, but I wanted to let you know anyway, and I will pull that meeting off the calendar for that week.
Cool.
Sounds good.
you.
All right.
Well, that is it on my end.
And I'll send a, I know there's a lot of action items, so I'll send a recap in Slack after this as well.
Well, yeah, appreciate that.
Sounds good.
Thank you.
Yeah.
All right.
Well, that's it on my end.
Anything else from you?
No, that should be all.
Thank you.
All right.
Talk to you soon.
Have a good one.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on Logic's strategy refresh and update content workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=67.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Messaging:** Logic is refining its core definition (e.g., "AI spec to production API platform") to be more concrete and avoid the vagueness of benefit-only taglines.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)
  - [**Content Automation:** With Logic flows fixed, the content pipeline will be automated. Azzam will review the logic doc this week to ensure it aligns with the new positioning.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=77.0)
  - [**Competitive Framing:** The strategy for comparison content is now "80% of the capability with 1% of the effort," positioning Logic as a practical alternative to complex frameworks like Llama Index.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=671.0)
  - [**Strategic Cadence:** Growth X will deliver the new editorial strategy this week, followed by a keyword audit and topic generation next week.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=912.0)

## Topics

### Core Messaging & Positioning

  - [**Problem:** The current tagline, "Be AI native," is too abstract and fails to clearly define Logic's function.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=353.0)
  - [**Solution:** Develop a concise, concrete definition.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)
      - [**Azzam's Ideas:** "AI spec to production API platform" or "description to production API platform."](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)
      - [**Rationale:** A clear identity is critical for marketing and will be used consistently across all articles.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)
  - [**Action:** Azzam will finalize the definition with Logic's team this week.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)

### Competitive Strategy

  - [**Goal:** Develop a nuanced positioning strategy for comparison content.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=621.0)
  - [**Category 1 (Complementary):** Tools like Zapier.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=679.0)
      - [**Positioning:** Complementary, not competitive.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=679.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Enables non-developers (e.g., technical ops) to use Logic via a familiar interface.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=679.0)
  - [**Category 2 & 3 (Alternative):** AI/LLM frameworks (Llama Index, Haystack) and cloud AI services.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=698.0)
      - [**Positioning:** "80% of the capability with 1% of the effort."](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=738.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Avoids triggering a "BS sensor" in CTOs by acknowledging Logic doesn't offer the same deep customization, but argues that most users don't need it.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=753.0)
  - [**Action:** Growth X will create a new artifact detailing this strategy for Azzam's review.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=621.0)

### Content Operations & Automation

  - [**Problem:** Logic flows were broken, blocking automation.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=77.0)
  - [**Resolution:** Steve (Logic) has fixed the flows.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=77.0)
  - [**Opportunity:** Automate the content pipeline (FAQ generation, logic workflows, auto-publishing).](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=85.0)
  - [**Blocker:** The logic doc, which dictates workflow selection, may need updates to align with the new positioning.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)
  - [**Action:** Azzam will review the logic doc and provide updates this week.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)

### Editorial Strategy & Timeline

  - [**New Editorial Strategy:** Growth X is finalizing a new strategy document.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=838.0)
      - [**Contents:** New topic clusters, keyword research approach, KPIs, and goals for search/AI visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=866.0)
      - [**Status:** Under internal review by Growth X directors; will be delivered to Azzam this week.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=838.0)
  - [**Content Cadence:**](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=912.0)
      - [**This Week:**](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=912.0)
          - [Deliver new editorial strategy (Growth X).](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=838.0)
          - [Finalize core definition (Azzam).](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)
          - [Review logic doc for automation (Azzam).](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)
      - [**Next Week:**](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=983.0)
          - [Audit existing keywords → identify articles for rewrite/reframe.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=983.0)
          - [Conduct new keyword research → present new topics.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=983.0)
          - [Update Scrunch dashboard and Looker prompts.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=983.0)
      - [**Following Weeks:**](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=1010.0)
          - [Run calibration articles to fine-tune the new pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=1010.0)

### Housekeeping

  - [**Feature Mentions:** Remove "Slack integration coming soon" from all articles.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=466.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Avoids outdated information, as the feature's timeline is uncertain.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=491.0)
  - [**Persona Prioritization:** Confirmed the content focus.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=525.0)
      - [**1. Primary:** CTO](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=525.0)
      - [**2. Secondary:** Engineering/Developer](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=525.0)
      - [**3. Tertiary:** CEO Enablement](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=525.0)
  - [**Holiday Closure:** Both teams will be closed from Christmas to New Year's. The weekly sync is canceled for that week.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=1061.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Azzam:**](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=1084.0)
      - [Finalize Logic's core definition with the internal team (due this week).](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)
      - [Review the logic doc for automation and provide updates (due this week).](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)
      - [Review the competitor list and comparison strategy artifact (when shared).](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=564.0)
  - [**Growth X:**](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=1084.0)
      - [Deliver the new editorial strategy document (due this week).](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=838.0)
      - [Share the competitor list and comparison strategy artifact for review.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=564.0)
      - [Remove "Slack integration coming soon" from all articles.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=466.0)
      - [Cancel the weekly sync for the holiday week.](https://fathom.video/share/RN2WHt4EZom5xqy2xhsqQ3h1zo8kyLXf?tab=summary&timestamp=1061.0)

action_items.description: Review Logic workflow/priority docs; send updates to Bailey
Draft Logic definition; validate w/ internal team; send to Bailey
Remove Slack integration mentions from all Logic content
Share competitors list + comparison positioning w/ Azzam; then create comparison artifact
Cancel Dec 22–Jan 2 weekly sync w/ Azzam
Post meeting recap in Slack for Azzam
